title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While visiting Helpsley Cemetery, Poirot reads the gravestone of Hugo Edmund Dreyfus, who died on March 20, 19xx. An elderly gardener approaches Poirot and shares that Mr. Dreyfus was a former athlete who became a cripple due to polio. The gardener describes the widow, Roya Alabella Dreyfus, as a bossy and highly active woman who managed many village associations and spent years caring for her husband. The gardener suggests that because she has no children and has completed her local responsibilities, she will likely move abroad to a place like Greece, Spain, or Portugal to find new things to manage.

Poirot asks the gardener for the location of Nicholas Lansham and Desmond Holland. The gardener explains that they live past the church in the third house on the left, where they are boarding with the Brandes. Poirot visits the two young men, aged eighteen and sixteen, who had assisted Mrs. Dribb with preparations for a Halloween party by climbing ladders, hanging lights, and placing golden pumpkins. Poirot observes that while they are well-mannered and dressed in expensive, modern clothing, they are the type of suspects the police and the gardener might target due to their age. Poirot engages them in conversation to seek their views on the case while observing their mannerisms and the work they performed during the party preparations.
